refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load cycle.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combines multiple cycle phases (load, travel, dump) into one requirement, which implies multiple obligations. To be atomic and solution-neutral, it is necessary to separate the cycle time targets for each phase to clearly define what the system must achieve without implying how.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the cycle time for the load phase under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions and verify that the 95th percentile cycle time (p95) is less than or equal to the customer-defined target.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load cycle.
The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the travel cycle.
The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the dump cycle.

candidate_dps: Optimize load cycle sequencing and timing
Implement adaptive cycle time control based on real-time feedback
Design load cycle with fixed timing parameters meeting target
Incorporate buffer and parallel processing to reduce cycle time
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive cycle time control based on real-time feedback

potential_sources: Dependency on real-time data acquisition and processing modules
Interaction with safety and operational policy enforcement modules
coupling_check.notes: Adaptive control logically decouples cycle time achievement from fixed parameters, allowing independent tuning and modular updates. Coupling risks arise mainly from integration with feedback sources and safety policies, which can be managed via clear interfaces.

assumptions_risks: Real-time feedback data is reliable and timely
Safety and operational policies do not impose constraints that conflict with adaptive control
Duty cycle and site conditions remain within expected ranges
Potential complexity in control logic may increase implementation risk
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining interfaces for feedback and safety policy modules to ensure modularity and reduce integration coupling.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for overall system control logic including adaptive cycle time control

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time feedback data for adaptive control

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated cycle time commands to execution modules

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as adaptive cycle time control is a core control function; minimal diffusion as it mainly interacts via telemetry and tasking interfaces.
Vehicle module is more about physical actuation; adaptive control logic would be diffused across vehicle subsystems.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control and monitoring, not internal adaptive control logic.
Safety Services focus on safety alerts and monitoring, not cycle time control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the adaptive cycle time control DP because it centralizes control logic and manages real-time feedback processing, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data which may be affected by network latency.
Risk of coupling with Tasking interface if cycle time commands are not well encapsulated.
If adaptive control logic grows complex, risk of NGHE Solution becoming a monolithic module.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks increase, consider decomposing adaptive cycle time control into a dedicated submodule within NGHE Solution to improve modularity without losing cohesion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Implement adaptive cycle time control based on real-time feedback' directly addresses the FR of supporting the customer-defined cycle time target by adapting control based on feedback, which aligns well with the acceptance criteria of achieving p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data which may be affected by network latency.
Risk of coupling with Tasking interface if cycle time commands are not well encapsulated.
If adaptive control logic grows complex, risk of NGHE Solution becoming a monolithic module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion (containment score 8) and minimal diffusion (diffusion score 2), indicating that the DP is well contained within a single module responsible for the adaptive cycle time control. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the containment boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through measuring the cycle time p95 against the CT_target under agreed duty cycle and site conditions, as specified in the acceptance criteria. The telemetry interface provides real-time feedback data necessary for adaptive control verification.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the FR to the NGHE Solution module is clear, as it owns the adaptive control logic and interfaces directly with telemetry and tasking modules, ensuring clear ownership and responsibility.

recommended_revisions: Monitor the complexity of the adaptive control logic within NGHE Solution to avoid monolithic growth; consider decomposing into dedicated submodules if needed.
Ensure robust encapsulation of cycle time commands in the Tasking interface to reduce coupling risks.
Mitigate telemetry data latency risks by implementing fallback or buffering strategies within NGHE Solution.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current level of decomposition sufficiently addresses the functional requirement with a well-contained design parameter and clear implementation module ownership. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed, and the acceptance criteria are met. Although there are some diffusion risks and complexity concerns, these are manageable through monitoring and mitigation strategies without necessitating further decomposition at this stage. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and contained module boundary is preferable to avoid unnecessary complexity.

revise.why: Monitor the complexity of the adaptive control logic within the NGHE Solution module to prevent monolithic growth and consider decomposing into submodules if complexity increases. Also, ensure robust encapsulation of cycle time commands and implement fallback strategies for telemetry data latency.

assumptions: Telemetry data will be sufficiently reliable and timely for adaptive control.
The NGHE Solution module can maintain high cohesion and low diffusion with current design.
Verification through cycle time p95 measurement is practical under operational conditions.
open_risks: Potential dependency on telemetry data latency affecting control performance.
Risk of coupling with Tasking interface if encapsulation is weak.
Possibility of NGHE Solution becoming monolithic if adaptive control logic complexity grows.
evidence_needed: Performance data showing cycle time p95 meets CT_target under real conditions.
Monitoring reports on module complexity and diffusion metrics.
Verification test results confirming adaptive control effectiveness and robustness.
